package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for filter_then_count_then_iterate: same collection traversed for filter, count, and processing passes.
type CaseFilterThenCountThenIterateDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

func PositiveFilterThenCountThenIterate(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseFilterThenCountThenIterateDTO) error {
    active := make([]CaseFilterThenCountThenIterateDTO, 0, len(items))
    for _, item := range items {
        if item.Status == input {
            active = append(active, item)
        }
    }

    activeCount := 0
    for range active {
        activeCount++
    }

    for _, item := range active {
        c.Set(item.ID, item.Amount+activeCount)
    }
    return nil
}
